questions.question: If an article is bought for Rs. 800 and sold at a profit of 30%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 36, 82, 16, 25
questions.explanation: 16, 25, and 36 are perfect squares. 82 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Pens are Pencils.
2. All Pencils are Stationery.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: Who wrote the national anthem of India?
Find the next number in the series: 5, 10, 20, 40, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 2. So, 40 * 2 = 80.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 28000 at an interest rate of 9% per annum over 4 years?
What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 35, 37, 39, 41, 43?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
A can complete a piece of work in 128 days while B can complete the same work in 240 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
If MAT is coded as OCV, how will PEN be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 2 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
